name: Abandoned World [Alpha Version]
description:
Once upon a time, the world was ruled by corporations... And it ended badly.

The world was destroyed. The corporations departed on spaceships, leaving ordinary people and small- and medium-sized businesses to die. As they departed, the corporations bombarded the planet with nuclear missiles from an orbital station, destroying civilization.

But a few organizations survived. Formerly corporations, quite large but betrayed by even larger corporations, they have developed greatly in the new world. Mostly, they are private military companies. Two stand out among them: Red Blood and Task Force. These two corporations rule. Red Blood is in the East, and Task Force is in the West. There are five other smaller corporations, still not small, but they don't count as major players and can't even suppress the chaos in their own territories. Hoping to take revenge on their former masters, they have declared Task Force their enemy.

The Red Bloods occupy the remains of major cities, and their headquarters? It's grown like a city. The once-powerful PMC has transformed into a militant state. They've adopted the ways of the new, brutal world—and become as strong as steel. Bureaucracy? It doesn't stifle them. Freedom? There's none, but there is security and ideology.

Task Force—once the main client of the wealthy bourgeoisie—has now lost its former dominance and is falling apart, but it still clings to the old order—oligarchy, freedom, and bureaucracy. Endless bureaucracy. Hoping for the return of the old masters, they obediently hold on.

And There is Raiders, killers, maniacs and mercyless animals, that resettled the Ruins of old cities. No any PMC ready to go in there...
